title: Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides
intro: Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides, also known as Mitrasacmoides, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae, commonly used in traditional medicine. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, and is used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of capsules, tablets, or tinctures.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides is classified within the family Rubiaceae. This family consists of a large number of species commonly known as coffee and madder family. The genus Oldenlandia is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides, along with other Oldenlandia species, is a member of the tribe Spermacoceae. The species Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides is native to parts of Asia.

The leaves of Oldenlandia mitrasacmoides are also said to be effective in reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.
